title: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
option_for_consideration: Explore the feasibility of establishing or contracting a security force to actively protect assets, property, and employees, and to rapidly respond to security incidents and/or emergencies. Depending on State and local regulations, security personnel may be subject to registration, licensing, certification, specific training, and other requirements.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-7, p. 9-5 to 9-7, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
option_for_consideration: Ensure that the size of the security force allows for adequate staffing of static posts and roving patrols.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-6 to 3-7, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
option_for_consideration: Ensure that the security force has the equipment necessary to meet the requirements established by security management (e.g., uniforms, communications, personal protective equipment).
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Officer Operations, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International, p. 17.

title: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
option_for_consideration: Ensure that the security force receives adequate, continuous training on topics such as emergency response, weapons and self-defense, SOPs, and screening and access.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-6, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
option_for_consideration: Provide the security force with comprehensive post orders and a dedicated command and control/operation center.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-6, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a security force. The lack of an adequately sized, well-trained, and well-equipped security force limits a facility's ability to respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
option_for_consideration: Create a surge capacity plan to augment the security force during special circumstances or elevate threats.
source_ref: Fischer, R.J., and G. Green, 2004, Introduction to Security, 7th Edition, Burlington, MA: Elsevier Inc., p. 48-53.

title: The facility does not have a surge capacity plan to provide additional security personnel during special circumstances or elevated threat.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a surge capacity plan to provide additional security personnel during special circumstances or elevated threat.
option_for_consideration: Establish a surge capacity plan to augment the security force during special circumstances, for example, during special events or natural disasters, and elevated threat situations. Options may include law enforcement officers (MOA, contract, and/or off-duty) or contract security (as part of an existing contract and/or another contract). Detail in the surge capacity plan the roles, responsibilities, and chain of command for both regular and surge forces.
source_ref: Fischer, R.J., and G. Green, 2004, Introduction to Security, 7th Edition, Burlington, MA: Elsevier Inc., p. 48-53.

title: The facility does not have an onsite security force. The company/organization employs a security force, but security personnel are not stationed at the facility. This limits the facility's ability to rapidly respond to security incidents and/or emergencies as they occur at the facility.
vulnerability: The facility does not have an onsite security force. The company/organization employs a security force, but security personnel are not stationed at the facility. This limits the facility's ability to rapidly respond to security incidents and/or emergencies as they occur at the facility.
option_for_consideration: Explore the feasibility of extending the security force to provide a continuous, onsite presence at the facility to actively protect assets, property, and employees, and to rapidly respond to security incidents and/or emergencies.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-7, p. 9-5 to 9-7, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not have the authority to temporarily detain persons suspected of criminal activity.
vulnerability: The security force does not have the authority to temporarily detain persons suspected of criminal activity.
option_for_consideration: Evaluate the facility's security requirements. If detainment authority is necessary, seek appropriate consultation, for example, with your legal department, contracted security firm, or local law enforcement for further guidance.
source_ref: Fischer, R.J., G. Green, 2004, Introduction to Security, 7th Edition, Burlington, MA: Elsevier Inc., p. 48.

title: The security force does not staff static posts at the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary (e.g., main entrance, loading dock).
vulnerability: The security force does not staff static posts at the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary (e.g., main entrance, loading dock).
option_for_consideration: Post security personnel at assets and areas where a continuous security force presence is necessary.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-7, p. 9-5 to 9-7, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not staff static posts at the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary (e.g., main entrance, loading dock).
vulnerability: The security force does not staff static posts at the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary (e.g., main entrance, loading dock).
option_for_consideration: Provide special instructions for each post, such as duties, staffing hours, and required equipment.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-8, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not staff static posts at the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary (e.g., main entrance, loading dock).
vulnerability: The security force does not staff static posts at the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary (e.g., main entrance, loading dock).
option_for_consideration: Establish a designated method of relief for security personnel assigned to static posts.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-5, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not staff static posts at most of the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary.
vulnerability: The security force does not staff static posts at most of the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary.
option_for_consideration: Explore ways to provide a continuous security force presence at the majority of the facility's SAAs and other areas as necessary.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-7, p. 9-5 to 9-7, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The duration of security force staffing at static posts is limited.
vulnerability: The duration of security force staffing at static posts is limited.
option_for_consideration: Determine if the duration of security force staffing at static posts meets the facility's security requirements. Extend shifts at static posts as necessary.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-5, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not conduct roving patrols that cover the facility's SAAs or other areas where monitoring and protection is necessary. If performed, roving patrols would allow the security force to more effectively monitor critical assets and areas, and deter, report, and respond to incidents.
vulnerability: The security force does not conduct roving patrols that cover the facility's SAAs or other areas where monitoring and protection is necessary. If performed, roving patrols would allow the security force to more effectively monitor critical assets and areas, and deter, report, and respond to incidents.
option_for_consideration: Ensure that SAAs and other areas as necessary are covered by roving patrols conducted by the security force. Routes and times for patrols should also be varied at frequent intervals to preclude establishing a routine that may be observed by potential intruders.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-5, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not conduct random roving patrols. Security patrols that are conducted in a regular, unvarying sequence may be observed and exploited.
vulnerability: The security force does not conduct random roving patrols. Security patrols that are conducted in a regular, unvarying sequence may be observed and exploited.
option_for_consideration: Vary routes and times of roving patrols at frequent intervals to preclude establishing a routine that may be observed by potential intruders.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-5, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not conduct roving patrols that cover most of the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary.
vulnerability: The security force does not conduct roving patrols that cover most of the facility's SAAs or other areas where entry control, monitoring, and/or protection is necessary.
option_for_consideration: Explore ways to provide roving patrols for a majority of the facility's SAAs and other areas as necessary.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-5, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The frequency of roving patrols is limited; therefore, detection and incident response at facility SAAs and other critical areas may be delayed.
vulnerability: The frequency of roving patrols is limited; therefore, detection and incident response at facility SAAs and other critical areas may be delayed.
option_for_consideration: Determine if the frequency of roving patrols meets the facility's security requirements for SAAs and other critical areas. Increase the  frequency of patrols as necessary.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-5, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not wear uniforms. An employee or security officer in uniform provides a visible security presence.
vulnerability: The security force does not wear uniforms. An employee or security officer in uniform provides a visible security presence.
option_for_consideration: Provide recognizable security uniforms to the guard force.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Officer Operations, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International, p. 17.

title: For special events, the facility does not use canines trained to detect IEDs to sweep protected area.
vulnerability: For special events, the facility does not use canines trained to detect IEDs to sweep protected area.
option_for_consideration: Coordinate with local, State, and/or Federal agencies (such as, local or State law enforcement, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives) regarding the use of their explosive detection canines for sweeps.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 1-4, p. 7-7, p. 9-4, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not have communications devices. Communications equipment is essential for the safe and efficient operation of the security force and the success of its assigned mission.
vulnerability: The security force does not have communications devices. Communications equipment is essential for the safe and efficient operation of the security force and the success of its assigned mission.
option_for_consideration: Equip the security force with the necessary communication devices to carry out their roles and responsibilities.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Officer Operations, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International, p. 17.

title: The security force does not have communications devices. Communications equipment is essential for the safe and efficient operation of the security force and the success of its assigned mission.
vulnerability: The security force does not have communications devices. Communications equipment is essential for the safe and efficient operation of the security force and the success of its assigned mission.
option_for_consideration: Provide two-way radios with secure-voice capability, if possible, to the security force.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Officer Operations, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International, p. 17.

title: The security force does not have communications devices. Communications equipment is essential for the safe and efficient operation of the security force and the success of its assigned mission.
vulnerability: The security force does not have communications devices. Communications equipment is essential for the safe and efficient operation of the security force and the success of its assigned mission.
option_for_consideration: Provide additional communications equipment to the security force, such as cell phones, duress alarms, and/or panic buttons.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-5, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not receive any type of training. A robust security force training program can decrease mistakes, increase proficiency, and enhance a facility's overall security posture.
vulnerability: The security force does not receive any type of training. A robust security force training program can decrease mistakes, increase proficiency, and enhance a facility's overall security posture.
option_for_consideration: Develop or contract a formal training program for the security force that covers topics such as emergency response, SOPs (for example, facility-specific SOPs, ICS/NIMS), screening and access, and, if appropriate, weapons and self-defense. Continuation/in-service training should be provided to maintain and enhance job proficiency. Document training and retain training records.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-6, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not receive any type of training. A robust security force training program can decrease mistakes, increase proficiency, and enhance a facility's overall security posture.
vulnerability: The security force does not receive any type of training. A robust security force training program can decrease mistakes, increase proficiency, and enhance a facility's overall security posture.
option_for_consideration: Employ security professionals certified or licensed through a Federal or State program. Ensure the training requirements they fulfilled to become certified/licensed will equip them to fulfill the facility's security requirements.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 9-6, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not receive continuous/in-service training. Refresher training helps security force personnel maintain continued proficiency and current knowledge of security issues, trends, and technologies.
vulnerability: The security force does not receive continuous/in-service training. Refresher training helps security force personnel maintain continued proficiency and current knowledge of security issues, trends, and technologies.
option_for_consideration: Provide or contract refresher training for the security force, at least annually. Document refresher training and retain training records.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Officer Operations, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International, p. 92-93.

title: The facility does not have comprehensive post orders for the security force, which may result in situations where security personnel do not fulfill their duties and/or perform outside the scope of their duties.
vulnerability: The facility does not have comprehensive post orders for the security force, which may result in situations where security personnel do not fulfill their duties and/or perform outside the scope of their duties.
option_for_consideration: Develop comprehensive post orders and make them available at each guard station.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-8, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The facility does not have comprehensive post orders for the security force, which may result in situations where security personnel do not fulfill their duties and/or perform outside the scope of their duties.
vulnerability: The facility does not have comprehensive post orders for the security force, which may result in situations where security personnel do not fulfill their duties and/or perform outside the scope of their duties.
option_for_consideration: Develop post orders that describe in detail the duties and responsibilities of the security force. After comprehensive post orders are developed, ensure that they are kept current and accessible. Comprehensive post orders may serve to express the policies of the protected enterprise, summarize required officer duties, avoid problems associated with of word-of-mouth instructions, and provide a basis for site-specific training. They are the vital link between the requirements of the facility and the ability of security personnel to effectively meet those requirements.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 3-8, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The security force does not have a dedicated command and control/operation center. Without a dedicated security center, the security force lacks a control point for coordination, monitoring and dispatch, and other critical activities.
vulnerability: The security force does not have a dedicated command and control/operation center. Without a dedicated security center, the security force lacks a control point for coordination, monitoring and dispatch, and other critical activities.
option_for_consideration: Establish a command and control post/operation center for the security force.
source_ref: Department of the Army, 2010, ATTP 3-39.32 (FM 3-19.30) – Physical Security, p. 6-14, http://fas.org/irp/doddir/army/attp3-39-32.pdf, accessed June 19, 2014.

title: The facility does not have a designated security manager. The lack of a security manager may hinder the application of security policies, programs, or procedures.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a designated security manager. The lack of a security manager may hinder the application of security policies, programs, or procedures.
option_for_consideration: Designate an employee to act as a security manager and task that person with developing, implementing, and coordinating all security-related activities. If possible, choose an employee with previous security experience. Consult The Critical Success Factor Method: Establishing a Foundation for Enterprise Security Management, available at http://www.ready.gov/document/critical-success-factor-method-establishing-foundation-enterprise-security-management-0, for more information.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Crisis Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not have a written security plan.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a written security plan.
option_for_consideration: Develop a comprehensive security plan specific to the facility. Train personnel on the plan, and exercise the plan at least once a year. Consult The Critical Success Factor Method: Establishing a Foundation for Enterprise Security Management, available at http://www.ready.gov/document/critical-success-factor-method-establishing-foundation-enterprise-security-management-0, for more information.
source_ref: Garcia, M.L., 2008, The Design and Evaluation of Physical Protection Systems, 2nd Edition, Burlington, MA: Elsevier Inc.

title: The facility does not have a written security plan.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a written security plan.
option_for_consideration: Develop a comprehensive security plan specific to the facility. The plan should address issues such as the following: protection of employees, contractors/vendors, visitors, and executives; protection of sensitive information; protection of funds; facility access control procedures; suspicious activity reporting procedures; employee termination procedures; parking; mail security; background checks; prohibited items; security force; end-of-day security checks; control and accountability of equipment (including keys); electronic security systems (including CCTV); physical security inspection programs; and employee security awareness training programs. Train personnel on the plan, and exercise the plan at least once a year.

title: The facility does not have a written security plan.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a written security plan.
option_for_consideration: Develop a comprehensive security plan specific to the facility. The plan should address topics such as the following: physical security measures and systems, security force, access control procedures, information protection, and security awareness training. In addition, the plan should include elements such as the following: an assessment of possible security risks; a review of threats to the facility and the facility's vulnerabilities; an up-to-date point of contact roster for key personnel responsible for security and first responders; and identification of critical assets or areas. Train personnel on the plan, and exercise the plan at least once a year.

title: The facility does not have a written security plan.
vulnerability: The facility does not have a written security plan.
option_for_consideration: Consult The Critical Success Factor Method: Establishing a Foundation for Enterprise Security Management, available at http://www.ready.gov/document/critical-success-factor-method-establishing-foundation-enterprise-security-management-0, for more information.

title: The corporate-level security plan does not reflect facility-specific needs.
vulnerability: The corporate-level security plan does not reflect facility-specific needs.
option_for_consideration: Develop a facility-specific addendum to the corporate security plan.
source_ref: Garcia, M.L., 2008, The Design and Evaluation of Physical Protection Systems, 2nd Edition, Burlington, MA: Elsevier Inc.; Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The corporate-level security plan does not reflect facility-specific needs.

title: Senior management personnel have not approved the facility's security plan, which suggests a possible lack of buy-in or oversight of security concerns.

option_for_consideration: Coordinate the security plan with local law enforcement. source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

option_for_consideration: Establish a liaison and regular communication with local law enforcement and other first responders, State and Federal law enforcement and counterterrorism agencies, and industry organizations to engage in information sharing, clarify response actions, track threat conditions, and support investigations.

title: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
vulnerability: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
option_for_consideration: Develop procedures for handling suspicious packages.

title: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
vulnerability: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
option_for_consideration: Incorporate procedures on how to handle suspicious packages into initial and annual security training programs.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
vulnerability: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
option_for_consideration: Implement U.S. Postal Service mail center security procedures to assess, prevent, and respond to threats such as suspicious letters and packages; bomb threats; and chemical, biological, or radiological contamination.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
vulnerability: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
option_for_consideration: Prominently display informational materials on suspicious package indicators in the mailroom.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
vulnerability: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
option_for_consideration: Develop a protocol to prevent air contamination (e.g., turn off fans or ventilation units, shut down the air handling system in the facility). Refer to the Ready.gov Website for more information about biological and chemical threats, at http://www.ready.gov/biological-threats, and http://www.ready.gov/chemical-threats.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
vulnerability: The facility lacks procedures for handling suspicious packages.
option_for_consideration: Refer to the Ready.gov Website for information about suspicious packages and letters, at http://www.ready.gov/explosions.

title: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
vulnerability: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
option_for_consideration: Develop a communication plan that covers voice and data transfer of security-related information to all personnel.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
vulnerability: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
option_for_consideration: Develop a process for communicating the current security situation and reminders of appropriate response procedures to all personnel. Include procedures for communicating with personnel who are not on site during an incident.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
vulnerability: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
option_for_consideration: Enhance security awareness through the distribution of security-related newsletter and emails, and the display of security-related information (e.g., posters) that address potential threats, training opportunities, initiatives, and concerns.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
vulnerability: The facility does not communicate security information to personnel.
option_for_consideration: Regularly test notification protocols through drills and exercises.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: There is no capability or process for an employee to report a security concern.
vulnerability: There is no capability or process for an employee to report a security concern.
option_for_consideration: Establish a system for reporting security concerns. Include mechanisms for the documentation, investigation, and review of related incidents and actions taken.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Security Management, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: There is no capability or process for an employee to report a security concern.
vulnerability: There is no capability or process for an employee to report a security concern.
option_for_consideration: Provide one or more options (e.g., hotline, direct phone or radio communications with security operations) for personnel to report security concerns (e.g., suspicious activity, potential threat, incident).

title: The facility does not participate in any security working groups.
vulnerability: The facility does not participate in any security working groups.
option_for_consideration: Join relevant security working groups. Establish liaisons and regular communications with Federal, State, local, private sector, and/or industry working groups to enhance information sharing, track threat conditions, and support investigations.

title: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on all employees.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
option_for_consideration: Conduct initial and recurring background checks on all employees.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
option_for_consideration: Conduct initial and recurring background checks on all employees working in positions of trust or who are assigned to critical or sensitive areas.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct any background checks.
option_for_consideration: Require contracting companies, temporary employment agencies, and/or vendors to conduct background checks on their personnel who will work at and/or visit the facility and to make such records available for audit.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although the facility conducts background checks, the type of checks conducted provides minimal information, creating a false sense of security in the workplace.
vulnerability: Although the facility conducts background checks, the type of checks conducted provides minimal information, creating a false sense of security in the workplace.
option_for_consideration: Explore options to conduct more comprehensive background checks to gather detailed information about personnel, especially those working in positions of trust or who are assigned to critical or sensitive areas.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although the facility conducts background checks, the type of checks conducted provides minimal information, creating a false sense of security in the workplace.
vulnerability: Although the facility conducts background checks, the type of checks conducted provides minimal information, creating a false sense of security in the workplace.
option_for_consideration: Conduct national criminal history background checks to thoroughly investigate personnel. Review credit reports especially for personnel in financial management positions where there is potential for fraud and embezzlement.
source_ref: ASIS International, 2014, Auditing Management Systems: Risk, Resilience, Security, and Continuity—Guidance for Application (ANSI/ASIS SPC.2-2014), Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although the facility conducts background checks, the type of checks conducted provides minimal information, creating a false sense of security in the workplace.
vulnerability: Although the facility conducts background checks, the type of checks conducted provides minimal information, creating a false sense of security in the workplace.
option_for_consideration: Seek appropriate consultation, for example, with your legal department, for guidance on conducting comprehensive background checks.

title: The facility does not conduct background checks on all employees.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct background checks on all employees.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on all employees.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not conduct background checks on all employees.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct background checks on all employees.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on all employees, including temporary employees.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not conduct background checks on all employees.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct background checks on all employees.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on all employees, not only employees identified as critical to facility operations.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although the facility conducts background checks on employees, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
vulnerability: Although the facility conducts background checks on employees, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
option_for_consideration: Conduct recurring background check on employees.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although the facility conducts background checks on employees, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
vulnerability: Although the facility conducts background checks on employees, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
option_for_consideration: Conduct recurring background checks on employees as appropriate. For example, recurring background checks may be necessary for employees working in positions of trust or assigned to critical or sensitive areas. Recurring background checks may occur on an annual basis, randomly, or on special occasions (e.g., when an employee is eligible for promotion).
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility does not conduct background checks on employee security personnel.
vulnerability: The facility does not conduct background checks on employee security personnel.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on security personnel.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although the facility conducts background checks on employee security personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
vulnerability: Although the facility conducts background checks on employee security personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
option_for_consideration: Conduct recurring background checks on security personnel.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Background checks are not conducted on contract security personnel.
vulnerability: Background checks are not conducted on contract security personnel.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on contract security personnel.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Background checks are not conducted on contract security personnel.
vulnerability: Background checks are not conducted on contract security personnel.
option_for_consideration: Require security contracting companies to conduct background checks on their personnel who will work at the facility and to make such records available for audit.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although background checks are conducted on contact security personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
vulnerability: Although background checks are conducted on contact security personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
option_for_consideration: Ensure recurring background checks are conducted on contract security personnel.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Background checks are not conducted on contractors.
vulnerability: Background checks are not conducted on contractors.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on all contractors.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Background checks are not conducted on contractors.
vulnerability: Background checks are not conducted on contractors.
option_for_consideration: Conduct background checks on contractors, especially those working in positions of trust or assigned to critical or sensitive areas.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Background checks are not conducted on contractors.
vulnerability: Background checks are not conducted on contractors.
option_for_consideration: Require contracting companies to conduct background checks on their personnel who will work at the facility and to make such records available for audit.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although background checks are conducted on contactors personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
vulnerability: Although background checks are conducted on contactors personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
option_for_consideration: Ensure recurring background checks are conducted on contractors.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although background checks are conducted on contactors personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
vulnerability: Although background checks are conducted on contactors personnel, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
option_for_consideration: Ensure recurring background checks are conducted on contractors as appropriate. For example, recurring background checks may be necessary for contractors working in positions of trust or assigned to critical or sensitive areas. Recurring background checks may occur on an annual basis, randomly, or on special occasions (e.g., when an contractor is eligible for reassignment).
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Background checks are not conducted on vendors.
vulnerability: Background checks are not conducted on vendors.
option_for_consideration: Require vendors to conduct background checks on their personnel who will work at the facility. Obtain proof from vendors that appropriate background checks have been conducted.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: Although background checks are conducted on vendors, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
vulnerability: Although background checks are conducted on vendors, checks are conducted only at initial hire. Recurring background checks are not conducted.
option_for_consideration: Require vendors to conduct recurring background checks on their personnel who will work at the facility. Obtain proof from vendors that recurring background checks are conducted.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Protection of Assets: Investigation, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility uses and maintains sensitive information but does not identify or protect it.
vulnerability: The facility uses and maintains sensitive information but does not identify or protect it.
option_for_consideration: Identify, mark, and protect sensitive information. Consult Building Security in the Digital Economy, available at  http://www.ready.gov/sites/default/files/documents/files/building_security_in_digital_economy.pdf, and Protecting Aggregated Data, available at  http://www.ready.gov/document/protecting%C2%A0aggregated%C2%A0data, for more information.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Information Security, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.
vulnerability: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.
option_for_consideration: Secure all sensitive information as appropriate (e.g., locked file cabinets, locked room).
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Information Security, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.
vulnerability: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.
option_for_consideration: Restrict access to sensitive information, for example, with password protection.
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Information Security, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.
vulnerability: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.
option_for_consideration: Create policies and procedures to adequately destroy sensitive information (e.g., shredding, burning).
source_ref: Walsh, T.J., and R.J. Healy, 2011, Information Security, M. Knoke, Ed., Alexandria, VA: ASIS International.

title: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.

option_for_consideration: Apply markings to sensitive information to identify its protection requirements.

title: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.

title: The facility utilizes, maintains, and identifies sensitive information but does not protect it in any manner. As a result, this information may be easily obtainable in person or online.

title: The facility does not store sensitive information securely. As a result, this information may be easily accessible.

option_for_consideration: Secure all sensitive information as appropriate (e.g., locked file cabinets, locked room). Consult Protecting Aggregated Data, available at http://www.ready.gov/document/protecting%C2%A0aggregated%C2%A0data, for more information.

title: The facility does not limit electronic access to sensitive information through the use of passwords. As a result, this information may be easily accessible.

title: The facility does not ensure the secure transmission of sensitive information.

title: The facility does not require a security review of information disseminated to the public (e.g., Internet postings).
